analysis_description: Tape-based IV (Brentq from mid vs extract mid), model IV from global quadratic smile in m=log(K/S)/sqrt(T), BS vega; L1 spread means; vega_rail grid mapping rail constant to max |pos| per strike at mid-session sample. TTE/DTE: round3work/round3description.txt (historical day index maps to TTE at open 8,7,6 for days 0,1,2 per spec example pattern) plus intraday DTE_eff = DTE_open - (timestamp//100)/10000 per round3work/plotting/original_method/combined_analysis/plot_iv_smile_round3.py (T = DTE_eff/365 years).
used_to_decide: Informed vega_rail magnitude (order-of-hundreds in vega proxy per unit position) and confirmed ATM-focused behavior is safer than flooding deep strikes (see iteration 1). Smile coeffs reused from round3work/voucher_work/5200_work/calibration.json global fit.

analysis_description: Implicit parameter sweep from backtests: IV_SCALPING_THR vs wing PnL (v1 low gate -> large losses on VEV_4000/4500); v2 ATM-only + no flatten reduces trade count but does not fix extract drag.
used_to_decide: Reverted toward stricter Frankfurt gate; kept order merge from v1 as infrastructure fix in v3; next iterations should parent v3 and tune day-2 hydrogel/extract risk or scalp thresholds narrowly.

analysis_description: IV/Greek signal-frequency by strike bucket (ATM/mid/wings) from ROUND_3 tapes using smile-IV BS theo/vega and Frankfurt theo-diff EMA windows. Compared edge trigger hit rates at THR_OPEN=0.45 and switch statistics to explain sparse fills.
used_to_decide: Observed ATM has materially tighter spreads and higher edge-hit rates than wings while wings have very wide spreads and low trigger rates; this justified pivoting away from underlying/hydro MM drag and testing pure vega-rail voucher execution (v4/v5).

analysis_description: Interpreting v6/v7 in light of vega_signal_window_stats.json: ATM q75 switch is ~0.27 (below Frankfurt gate 0.7), so most of the time the quiet flatten branch or no-fill dominates; v7 lowered IV gate toward q75 to increase use of the main branch but reintroduced small wing/extract hedge cost (VEV_5500, extract), net negative vs v5.
used_to_decide: Best candidate remains v5 (+14 total PnL). v6 ATM filter kept strategy safe but thinned to +2. Next parents: v5 for threshold tuning around switch_mean and THR_OPEN without dropping IV gate as far as v1.

analysis_description: Subsampled tape distribution of the Frankfurt switch_mean (EMA_100 of |theo_diff - EMA_20|) per voucher row; TTE and smile as traders. Output: share of rows where switch_mean >= 0.55/0.65/0.7 (rare) and quantiles 0.5-0.99. This explains why IV_SCALPING_THR=0.7 rarely opens the main branch and why v8 (no extract hedge) ties v5 on total PnL.
used_to_decide: v8 duplicate of v5 PnL (+14) with no extract: hedge was not the edge driver. v9 (slightly looser gate) was worse (-8) due to extract hedge drag + more wing strikes. Prefer v5 or v8 as parent; if tuning IV gate, use small steps above tape median (~0.23) and below 0.55 unless willing to change EMA window shape.

analysis_description: v10 set IV gate to 0.25 to align with subsampled switch_mean median (~0.23). That let the *main* Frankfurt scalper run on all strikes, not the quiet-flatten path — wings (VEV_4000/4500) dominated losses. v11 kept gate 0.7 but set QUIET_FLATTEN off: when switch_mean is low, no more forced take/stop of full inventory at the touch (reduces spread-driven hidden liquidation PnL). v11 PnL +96 vs v8 +14, consistent with prior v2 ‘no quiet flatten’ direction but now with v8’s no-hedge baseline.
used_to_decide: Do **not** use tape median as IV activity gate for the *main* branch without strike filters — it replicates v1-style wing damage. Next parents: v11; optional combine with ATM filter or slightly higher gate than 0.25 for any main-branch experiments.

analysis_description: ATM vs non-ATM switch/edge analysis from ROUND_3 tapes using trader-consistent smile+TTE+EMA formulas (output atm_switch_vs_edge.json). ATM subset shows much tighter spreads and stronger edge-open hit rates than non-ATM, while high switch_mean remains rare overall; this supports combining no-quiet-flatten with ATM-only exposure.
used_to_decide: Guided v12 (ATM filter + no quiet flatten) which improved PnL to +130. v13 showed over-tight vega rail sacrifices too much edge; keep rail near 650 with ATM filter.

analysis_description: Grid around v12: (v14) widening ATM 0.96-1.04 added marginal strike access but PnL fell to +96 (consistent with vega_signal_window_stats where mid-bucket is riskier than tight ATM). (v15) VEGA_RAIL 700 vs 650: near-tie at +128 vs +130 with slightly worse day-1 frictions (-3 vs -2).
used_to_decide: Keep **v12** as the leading recipe (QUIET_FLATTEN off, 0.97-0.03 ATM, VEGA_RAIL 650). Next parents: v12; optional micro-sweeps on THR_OPEN only, not widened ATM, unless combining with a wing-exclusion list.

analysis_description: Vega concentration over strikes/time (BS vega weights from tape + TTE rules): top-strike share q95 ~0.254, HHI q95 ~0.183; concentration increases into day2 but remains moderate (no extreme single-strike domination). Added family-12 execution coupling test: stronger skew only when concentration threshold crossed. Also captured full backtest logs + submission-only fill summaries for v16/v17.
used_to_decide: Because concentration is moderate (HHI << 0.4), aggressive concentration gating gave limited differentiation and did not beat v12 (+130). Family-12 pivot is validated as distinct in implementation, but currently weaker under worse fills; v12 remains best versus our branch and far below 800k benchmark.

analysis_description: REQUIRED duplicate-check against parent index 12 prompted variant adjustment. Switched from market-HHI threshold skew (v16/v17) to a distinct own-inventory-vega concentration coupling (v18/v19): continuous skew by each strike's share of current portfolio vega. Kept family12 spline-logK smile and minimal other changes.
used_to_decide: Distinct variant is implemented and tested, but performance remains 75 (< v12=130 and far below 800k benchmark). Parent-leading recipe still v12.

analysis_description: Priority propagation analysis requested: measured per-contract response dm_v to extract move du (beta, lead/lag beta_lag1, spread-change conditional on large |du| shocks) in extract_move_propagation_by_contract.json. Findings: strong immediate sensitivity in lower strikes (4000-5100), near-zero lag1, and little positive spread widening in top-response bucket. Used to design risk logic: overweight inventory capacity where propagation is strongest and avoid opening when spread widens after extract shocks.
used_to_decide: v20/v21 were logical but too restrictive: only 2 submission trade events each and +79 total vs v12 +130. Keep v12 leader; future propagation logic should modulate edge/size continuously rather than hard skipping to avoid starving fills.

analysis_description: Extended propagation (extract_propagation_extended.json, run_extract_propagation_extended.py): on each tick, du_extract, dmid per VEV, d(IV) from the same quadratic smile as trading vega, BS vega. Confirms OLS beta is stable (up vs down within ~0.2% of all-beta) and d(IV) on du is tiny vs price moves; 90p |du| is only 2 units so 'shock' gating misfired in v20. v22/v23 apply continuous per-strike THR_OPEN = THR_OPEN * clamp(BETA_REF/beta_K) to favor high-beta 5100/5200/5400 flow without hard skips; Greek thread unchanged (smile + bs_vega, vega rail).
used_to_decide: Replaces v20 spread-shock hard skip with soft threshold scaling from tape betas. v22 and v22-clamp (v23) both reach +132 vs v12 +130; submission-side counts match prior ~3 (path dominated by one day). New leader: v22 (tie with v23; prefer v22 for stronger beta tilt).

analysis_description: Regime analysis after v22: day0/day1 still mostly inactive despite ATM availability. Practical bottleneck remains Frankfurt switch gate. To keep propagation thread intact, we retained beta-scaled THR_OPEN and swept IV_SCALPING_THR lower (0.55, 0.45) instead of adding hard skips. This is still Greek-linked (vega rail + smile theo diff), but now with higher flow.
used_to_decide: Lower switch gate improved submission events (3 -> 5) and PnL (+132 -> +136/+146), while preserving the propagation-based soft strike modulation. v25 is current best on branch.

analysis_description: Replicated the Frankfurt switch_ema (EMA of |option_theo_diff - mean_theo_diff|) for ATM VEV_5100 with the same smile/BS and EMA windows as the trader, per CSV day, in run_atm_switch_ema_tape.py -> atm_switch_ema_5100_by_day.json. p90~0.32-0.35, p95~0.33-0.36, median~0.28-0.30. So a gate of 0.7 is a rare event; 0.45 and 0.30 sit near/above the tape distribution, not a tail.
used_to_decide: Swept IV_SCALPING_THR: 0.40 (+140) < 0.45 v25 (+146) < tie 0.30 v27 (+146, more events). 0.35 was too open (+65) — not monotonic, model vs worse-fill path matters. No new PnL high; v25 and v27 remain best +146. Prefer v27 for higher submission count at same PnL.

analysis_description: Propagation-to-quoting extension: analyze du_ema (EMA of |du_extract|, window 100) vs ATM VEV_5100 edge |theo_diff-mean| in du_regime_vs_edge_5100.json. Edge generally increases with du_ema (notably day0/day1 top bucket p90), suggesting dynamic gate can be lowered in higher-motion regimes. Implemented continuous gate scaling in iv_scalp_orders: gate = IV_SCALPING_THR * clamp(1 - k*(du_ema-base), min, max).
used_to_decide: Dynamic gating preserved the v25 shape but did not exceed best PnL (v29 +142, v30 +144 vs best +146). Keep simpler fixed-gate leaders v25/v27 for tie-break simplicity.

analysis_description: Read round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt and outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt: joint BBO spread gate (VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 both ask-bid <= 2) aligns with more favorable short-horizon extract mid; spread-spread and book-state (Sonic/inclineGod). Replicated our own gate share per day: run_tight_5200_5300_stats.py -> r3_tight_5200_5300_gate_by_day.json. v31: parent v25 with continuous TIGHT_VEGA_ROOM_MULT when gate on, WIDE when off; no extra extract MM to respect mid-vs-execution caveat. IV vega-rail and smile unchanged.
used_to_decide: Joint gate integrated as continuous vega room scaling only. Days 0-2 only (3-0 3-1 3-2) after merge: worse PnL +177 vs v25 +146 on the same 3 days; all matches worse in this run (6 submission events each). Beware: bare '3' now backtests 4 days including new day-3 data.

analysis_description: Pivoted away from the branch’s legacy per-agent (Frankfurt/vega-rail) line. Authoritative read: round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt (post-merge) and ORIGINAL_DISCORD_QUOTES.txt (inclineGod: spreads per contract + spread correlation; Sonic: VEV flow gated on both 5200 and 5300 spread ≤ 2, cleaner n). outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt documents tight vs not-tight forward extract mid. Implemented pure thesis traders v32–v34: BBO spread gate (ask₁−bid₁) for VEV_5200+VEV_5300, no hydrogel; EMA mean-reversion execution at bid/ask. Backtest both --match-trades worse and all; store dual logs in results.json.
used_to_decide: v32: gate + extract + 5100/5200/5300 mean-rev → worse PnL strongly negative; STRATEGY mid-forward caveat applies. v33: VEV only (5100/5200/5300) → worse +13,898 over 4 R3 days. v34: gate legs only 5200+5300, slower EMA → best worse +19,335; all +19,522. None approach 200k/day floor; next pass should explore slower gates, sub-day regime, or inventory caps while keeping joint spread definition.
